quotes.text: SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE active = 1 ORDER BY name ASC;
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100 AND stock > 0 ORDER BY price DESC;
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS total FROM employees GROUP BY department;
SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING AVG(salary) > 50000;
SELECT u.name, o.total FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id;
SELECT u.name, o.total FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.id IS NULL;
SELECT p.name, c.name AS category FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id ORDER BY c.name;
INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('Alice', 'alice@example.com', NOW());
INSERT INTO orders (user_id, product_id, quantity, total) VALUES (1, 42, 3, 59.99);
INSERT INTO logs (event, message, created_at) SELECT 'login', name, NOW() FROM users WHERE active = 1;
UPDATE users SET email = 'newemail@example.com', updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = 5;
UPDATE products SET stock = stock - 1 WHERE id = 10 AND stock > 0;
UPDATE orders SET status = 'shipped' WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 2 DAY AND status = 'pending';
DELETE FROM sessions WHERE expired_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 7 DAY AND user_id IS NOT NULL;
DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 7 AND created_at < '2020-01-01' AND active = 0;
CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE orders (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, user_id INT NOT NULL, total DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL, status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'pending', FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
CREATE TABLE products (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL, stock INT DEFAULT 0, category_id INT, INDEX (category_id));
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users (email) USING BTREE; CREATE INDEX idx_users_name ON users (name);
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN phone VARCHAR(20) AFTER email, ADD COLUMN verified BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE;
ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN description TEXT, ADD COLUMN image_url VARCHAR(500);
ALTER TABLE orders ADD CONSTRAINT fk_user FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_logs; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_sessions; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_cache;
TRUNCATE TABLE sessions; TRUNCATE TABLE rate_limits; ALTER TABLE sessions AUTO_INCREMENT = 1;
SELECT name, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rank FROM employees;
SELECT id, name, SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY created_at ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS running_total FROM sales;
WITH high_value AS (SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE total > 500) SELECT u.name FROM users u JOIN high_value h ON u.id = h.user_id;

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (SELECT id, name, parent_id FROM categories WHERE parent_id IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT c.id, c.name, c.parent_id FROM categories c JOIN cte ON c.parent_id = cte.id) SELECT * FROM cte;
SELECT name, salary, CASE WHEN salary > 80000 THEN 'High' WHEN salary > 50000 THEN 'Mid' ELSE 'Low' END AS salary_band FROM employees;
SELECT id, COALESCE(phone, email, 'N/A') AS contact FROM users WHERE active = 1 ORDER BY id;
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE total > 1000);
SELECT name FROM products WHERE price > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM products) ORDER BY price ASC;
SELECT u.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count, SUM(o.total) AS total_spent FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.id, u.name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 10;
CREATE VIEW active_users AS SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE active = 1 AND last_login > NOW() - INTERVAL 30 DAY;
CREATE PROCEDURE get_user_orders(IN user_id INT) BEGIN SELECT o.id, o.total, o.status FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = user_id ORDER BY o.created_at DESC; END;
START TRANSACTION; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 500 WHERE id = 1; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 500 WHERE id = 2; COMMIT;
SELECT DATE_FORMAT(created_at, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(total) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month;
SELECT p.name, ROUND(AVG(r.rating), 2) AS avg_rating, COUNT(r.id) AS review_count FROM products p JOIN reviews r ON p.id = r.product_id GROUP BY p.id HAVING COUNT(r.id) >= 5 ORDER BY avg_rating DESC;
EXPLAIN SELECT u.name, COUNT(o.id) FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.created_at > '2024-01-01' GROUP BY u.id;
SELECT t1.id, t1.value, t2.value AS next_value FROM table1 t1 LEFT JOIN table1 t2 ON t2.id = t1.id + 1 ORDER BY t1.id;
